findings.evidence: `BaseChange.lean:126` has `{K R S : Type v}`; `BaseChange.lean:80` defines the underlying `AlgHom.baseChangePointsMulEquiv` over `{k K A R : Type*}`; `PointsFunctor.lean:50` has `points (A : CommAlgCat.{w} R) : GrpCat.{max v w}`, and Mathlib `CommAlgCat.of`/`GrpCat` definitions show the object universe can be chosen explicitly (`CommAlgCat/Basic.lean:55`, `Grp/Basic.lean:39,505`).
findings.fix: Use separate universes, e.g. `{K : Type v} {R S : Type x}`, and put both endpoints in an enlarged value category such as `(CommAlgCat.of k R : CommAlgCat.{max v x} k)` and `(CommAlgCat.of K R : CommAlgCat.{max v x} K)`, with the same explicit universe in the naturality lemmas.
findings.issue: `baseChangePointsIso` and its naturality lemmas declare `{K R S : Type v}`, forcing the extension algebra `K` and value algebras `R,S` to live in one universe; this is stronger than the categorical `GrpCat` packaging requires.

summary: The bundled base-change API is still less universe-polymorphic than the underlying equivalence and the points API allow. The remaining restriction ties the extension ring universe to the value-algebra universe instead of only enlarging the categorical target universe.
